stories.headline: Brightline Hurtles Toward Debt Deadline With No Plan in Place
stories.summary: It’s a date that lenders to Brightline, the debt-laden private Florida railroad, have had circled for months: July 1. That’s when the company will likely look to scrounge up what’s left in reserves to pay bondholders and stave off a formal default for the last time.

stories.headline: Ukraine’s former commander-in-chief to run for president, reports say
stories.summary: Valerii Zaluzhnyi, former commander-in-chief of Ukraine's Armed Forces and now Kyiv’s envoy to the UK, is reportedly willing to run in the next presidential election once the situation allows. Zaluzhnyi is seen as Zelenskyy’s main rival.

stories.headline: Ukraine hits Russia's Ufa oil refinery for second time in a week, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Ukraine’s domestically developed and manufactured drones and missiles have been hammering Russian oil facilities, including refineries, terminals, storage depots and pipeline pumping stations, for months.

Reference is made to the '12th blue dot,' a hypothetical addition to the Fed's dot plot that would represent a signal for rate cuts this year, attributed to former Fed official Warsh. This suggests that despite market expectations, JPMorgan analysts anticipate the Federal Reserve will keep rates on hold throu

stories.summary: The United United Arab Emirates has restored its oil exports to pre-war levels by sneaking tankers through the Strait of Hormuz undetected and using a pipeline that bypasses the waterway.

stories.headline: BlackRock Says Past Defaults, Next Election Cap Argentina Rally

stories.headline: Where Trump Won and Lost at the Supreme Court

stories.headline: Despite Some Losses for Trump, Supreme Court Delivers Enduring Conservative Wins
stories.summary: The justices pushed back on some of President Trump’s signature moves, but they also expanded presidential power and supplied victories on long-sought conservative goals.

stories.headline: Takeaways From the Colorado Primary Elections: Another Win for Democratic Socialists
stories.summary: A democratic socialist ousted a veteran congresswoman in Denver, and a U.S. senator lost his bid for governor. But the state’s other senator fended off a progressive primary challenger.

stories.headline: Small Caps Track Largest S&P 500 Lead Since 2003
stories.summary:
The Russell 2000 has outperformed the S&P 500 by almost 14 percentage points during the first six months of this year. Should such a performance gap hold into year-end, it would be the largest in over 20 years.
Jill Carey Hall, Equity Strategist and Head: US SMID Strategy at Bank of America, discusses her mid-year outlook for small cap stocks. (Source: Bloomberg)
